summaryParagraphs: इमेज स्रोत, ANI मेघालय के ईस्ट जयंतिया हिल्स कोयला खदान विस्फ़ोट केस में राज्य सरकार ने न्यायिक जांच आयोग बनाने का फ़ैसला किया है. ये जानकारी मेघालय के मुख्यमंत्री कॉनराड के संगमा ने दी है. उन्होंने एक्स पर पोस्ट किया, "सरकार ने ईस्ट जयंतिया हिल्स के मिन्संगाट-थांगस्को इलाके में हाल ही में हुई दुःखद माइनिंग घटना की जांच के लिए कमीशन ऑफ़ इन्क्वायरी एक्ट के तहत एक न्यायिक जांच आयोग बनाने का फ़ैसला किया है." पांच फ़रवरी को मेघालय के ईस्ट जयंति‍या हिल्स ज़िले में एक अवैध कोयला खदान में धमाका हुआ था. इस हादसे में 27 लोगों की मौत की पुष्टि हुई है. items.headline: Amid gloom, H-1B visas find new use as US tech giants hire AI talent amid crackdown
whyItMatters: New Delhi , UPDATED: Feb 9, 2026 12:40 IST Written By: Shounak Sanyal US technology companies' push into artificial intelligence is driving strong demand for H-1B visa holders in AI-related roles, acc
summaryParagraphs: New Delhi , UPDATED: Feb 9, 2026 12:40 IST Written By: Shounak Sanyal US technology companies' push into artificial intelligence is driving strong demand for H-1B visa holders in AI-related roles, according to a report by Forbes. This comes amid the Trump administration's crackdown on foreign hiring by tech companies, especially through the H-1B programme. The clampdown, however, works against the American government's plans to provide the US a leading edge in AI development. Immigration data analysed by the National Foundation for American Policy (NFAP) show that more than 80% of labour condition applications (LCA's) for new H-1B roles at Amazon, Meta, Google, Microsoft and Apple in FY2025 were tied to AI-related occupations. Citing this data, the Forbes report from Sunday (US time) stated that foreign-born professionals were now being deeply embedded in US tech firms' AI expansion strategies. H-1B visas remain one of the few pathways for highly skilled foreign professionals to work l For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Web.

summaryParagraphs: There is no fixed length or formula for success in the T20 format, but the smarter you are with your skill set, the better your chances of thriving Pratyush Raj is a cricket journalist with TOI Sports, specializing in all things cricket, both on and off the field. He began his career with the Business Standard before following his passion as a sports reporter for the Times of India in Chandigarh. Pratyush has also contributed to the sports coverage of India Today Group and his last stint was with The Indian Express. His love for sports was nurtured during his upbringing in flood-prone Saharsa, a district in North Bihar, where Cricket Samrat was his constant companion. items.headline: What is Mukhyamantri Ladki Bahin e-KYC deadline?
whyItMatters: Synopsis Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin: Women beneficiaries of the Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin scheme can now correct errors in their e-KYC process. The Maharashtra government has extended the dea
summaryParagraphs: Synopsis Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin: Women beneficiaries of the Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin scheme can now correct errors in their e-KYC process. The Maharashtra government has extended the deadline for these corrections to March 31, 2026. ET Online What is Mukhyamantri Ladki Bahin e-KYC deadline? Aditi Tatkare, Maharashtra’s women and child development minister, reiterated that Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin beneficiaries who made mistakes while completing their eKYC process can rectify their mistakes by March 31, 2026. Many Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin beneficiaries while completing their e-KYC process, selected the wrong options, leading to complaints and difficulties in receiving the scheme benefits. The government has received many complaints regarding this issue, especially for those eligible beneficiaries who have completed e-KYC but have not yet received the scheme's benefits, all such beneficiaries are being given another opportunity to correct their e-KYC inform For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Web.

items.headline: WhatsApp privacy case: SC defers hearing to Feb 23
whyItMatters: Synopsis The Supreme Court deferred to February 23 pleas by Meta Platforms Inc and WhatsApp against a CCI privacy penalty. A bench led by CJI Surya Kant adjourned the hearing after Kapil Sibal was sai
summaryParagraphs: Synopsis The Supreme Court deferred to February 23 pleas by Meta Platforms Inc and WhatsApp against a CCI privacy penalty. A bench led by CJI Surya Kant adjourned the hearing after Kapil Sibal was said to be unwell. The court said it would pass an interim order on February 23 then. ANI The Supreme Court on Monday deferred to February 23 pleas of Meta Platforms Inc and WhatsApp against a Competition Commission of India (CCI) order imposing a penalty of Rs 213.14 crore over privacy policy. A bench comprising Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and N V Anjaria was told that senior advocate Kapil Sibal was unwell and hence the hearing should be adjourned. The bench said it would take up the pleas for passing an interim order on February 23. It permitted a litigant represented by senior advocate Arvind Datar to be made a party to the case. On February 3, the bench made strong observations against Meta Platforms Inc and WhatsApp, saying they could not "p For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Web.
